aliases: United States Air Force Reserve Command
description: United States Air Force major command responsible for the federal air reserve component
description_wiki: The United States Air Force Reserve (USAFR, AFR, or AFRES) is one of the United States Air Force's (USAF) two air reserve components, alongside the Air National Guard (ANG).

description: human settlement in Bexar County, Texas, United States of America
description_wiki: Lackland Air Force Base  (IATA: SKF, ICAO: KSKF, FAA LID: SKF) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base located in Bexar County, Texas, United States.

description: city in Bexar, Comal, and Medina counties in Texas, United States, that is the seat of Bexar County
description_wiki: San Antonio ( SAN an-TOH-nee-oh; Spanish for "Saint Anthony") is a city in the U.S. state of Texas.

description: armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia between North Vietnam and South Vietnam, from 1955 to 1975
description_wiki: The Vietnam War (1 November 1955 – 30 April 1975) was an armed conflict in Vietnam, Laos, and Cambodia fought between North Vietnam (Democratic Republic of Vietnam) and South Vietnam (Republic of Vietnam) and their allies.

description: US Air Force base near Wichita Falls, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Sheppard Air Force Base (IATA: SPS, ICAO: KSPS, FAA LID: SPS) is a United States Air Force (USAF) base located five miles (8.0 km) north of the central business district of Wichita Falls, in Wichita County, Texas, United States.

description: city in and county seat of Wichita County, Texas, United States
description_wiki: Wichita Falls ( WITCH-ih-tah) is a city in and the county seat of Wichita County, Texas, United States.

description: public research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States
description_wiki: Purdue University is a public land-grant research university in West Lafayette, Indiana, United States, and the flagship campus of the Purdue University system, which also includes Purdue University in Indianapolis as an ongoing expansion of the West Lafayette campus.

aliases: Secretary of Defense of the United States
Secretary of War of the United States
description: head of the U.S. Department of Defense
description_wiki: The United States secretary of defense (SecDef), secondarily titled the secretary of war (SecWar), is the head of the United States Department of Defense (DoD), the executive department of the U.S. Armed Forces, and is a high-ranking member of the cabinet of the United States.

description: former US Air Force base near Columbus, Indiana
description_wiki: Bakalar Air Force Base is a former U.S. Air Force base located 4.4 miles (7.1 km) northeast of Columbus, Indiana.

The Fairchild C-119 Flying Boxcar (Navy and Marine Corps designation R4Q) is an American military transport aircraft developed from the World War II-era Fairchild C-82 Packet, designed to carry cargo, personnel, litter patients, and mechanized equipment, and to drop cargo and troops by parachute.

aliases: Northwest Training Center of the Army Air Corps
description: former U.S. Air Force base located in Ohio

aliases: The Biggest Small Town in America
description: capital city of Ohio, United States of America
description_wiki: Columbus ( kə-LUM-bəs) is the capital and most populous city of the U.S. state of Ohio.
Rickenbacker Air National Guard Base is an Ohio Air National Guard installation at Rickenbacker International Airport near Lockbourne in southern Franklin County.
The 71st Special Operations Squadron is part of the 58th Special Operations Wing at Kirtland Air Force Base, New Mexico.
The Douglas AC-47 Spooky (also nicknamed "Puff, the Magic Dragon") was the first in a series of fixed-wing gunships developed by the United States Air Force during the Vietnam War.

description: air service branch of the United States Armed Forces
description_wiki: The United States Air Force (USAF) is the air service branch of the United States Armed Forces.
